initiative.name: OpenIncident v0.3 - Multi-Source Alerts, Grouping & Routing
initiative.description: Extend alert ingestion beyond Prometheus to support Grafana, CloudWatch, and generic webhooks. Introduce a WebhookProvider abstraction so new sources can be added with minimal effort. Enhance alert deduplication for cross-source scenarios, add alert grouping rules (multiple alerts → one incident), and implement routing rules to direct alerts to the right teams and channels based on labels and metadata.
success_criteria: Grafana webhook fires → alert stored with source='grafana', incident auto-created
CloudWatch SNS notification → alert stored with source='cloudwatch', incident auto-created
Generic webhook with user-defined schema → alert stored, incident auto-created if severity matches
Duplicate alerts from any source are properly deduplicated using (source, external_id)
Alert grouping rules combine related alerts into a single incident (e.g., same service + 5min window)
Routing rules direct alerts to specific Slack channels or teams based on labels
Existing Prometheus webhook continues to work identically (backwards compatible)
New webhook sources can be added by implementing a single WebhookProvider interface
initiative.architecture_notes: Introduces WebhookProvider adapter pattern (Strategy pattern) mirroring the existing ChatService abstraction. Each source implements ParsePayload() → []NormalizedAlert. AlertService gains a generic ProcessNormalizedAlerts() method. Grouping and routing engines sit between normalization and incident creation in the pipeline.
